title: Putting it Together: The Optimizer
concept: The optimizer uses gradient descent to repeatedly adjust model weights to minimize the cost function during training.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the update rule for a weight in Gradient Descent?

options.text: New Weight = Old Weight - (Learning Rate * Slope)
New Weight = Old Weight + (Learning Rate * Slope)

exercises.explain: To go downhill, we subtract the product of the learning rate and the slope (gradient) from the old weight.

cards.title: The loop of learning
cards.body: Think of how a child learns to throw a ball. They throw, see how far off they were, adjust their arm angle, and throw again. They repeat this loop until they hit the target.

cards.title: The Optimizer
cards.body: An optimizer is the algorithm that updates the model's weights during training using the gradient step loop to minimize the cost function.

cards.title: The brain of training
cards.body: If the model is the body that makes guesses, the optimizer is the brain that learns from mistakes and decides how to adjust the muscles (weights) for the next try.

cards.title: The training cycle
cards.body: During training, we feed in data, calculate the loss, find the gradients, and let the optimizer update the weights. We repeat this cycle thousands of times (called epochs) until the error stops shrinking.

cards.title: More than basic GD
cards.body: While basic Gradient Descent is the foundation, modern optimizers (like Adam or SGD) are smarter. They adjust the learning rate on the fly to help models train faster and escape local minimums.

cards.title: The optimization loop
cards.body: How predictions and optimizations loop together during training:

left.text:
Model makes predictions.
Cost function measures how wrong the predictions were.

right.text:
Optimizer calculates slopes.
Updates weights to lower the error for the next round.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary role of an optimizer in machine learning?

options.text: To collect and clean the training dataset
To adjust model weights based on error gradients to minimize the cost function
To deploy the model to the cloud

exercises.explain: The optimizer is responsible for implementing the update rules to minimize the cost function over time.

exercises.prompt: True or false: The optimization loop runs only once during the entire training process.
exercises.explain: False! The optimizer runs iteratively over many epochs, repeatedly adjusting weights as it sees the data.

exercises.sentence: One complete pass of the entire training dataset through the optimization loop is called an ___.
exercises.explain: An epoch is one full cycle where the model sees all training data and updates its parameters accordingly.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is a commonly used modern optimization algorithm?
exercises.explain: Adam (Adaptive Moment Estimation) is one of the most popular and robust adaptive optimizers, building on the momentum and RMSprop ideas you just learned.

exercises.prompt: You are building a self-correcting thermostat. It measures how far the room temperature is from the target (loss) and updates its heating dial (weights). What acts as the optimizer here?

options.text: The algorithm that calculates the temperature error and adjusts the dial step-by-step to minimize it
The temperature sensor that measures the current heat

exercises.explain: Brilliant! The optimizer is the logic that takes the error, calculates the correction, and adjusts the parameters (the dial) iteratively. Congratulations on completing the Gradient Descent Fundamentals unit!
